skills.name: wiki-refresh
skills.description: Incrementally refresh docs/llm-wiki/ after code changes. Use when wiki is stale or after merging a feature branch.

skills.name: wiki-add-service
skills.description: Create a new service-doc page under docs/llm-wiki/wiki/services/ for a service that exists in the project but has no wiki page yet. Use when /wiki-refresh surfaces a missing-service suggestion.

skills.name: repo-fanout-pr
skills.description: Fans out a multi-repo change into one commit + push + PR per affected child repo and cross-links the PR bodies. Invoked by /implement-ticket Phase 9 when the workspace contains more than one git repo. Not for direct user use.

skills.name: mastering-git-cli
skills.description: Git version control operations: git status, git log, git diff, commits, branches, merge conflicts, rebase, stash, cherry-pick, git history, worktrees, submodules, and repository troubleshooting. Use for git commands, branch management, merge strategies, conflict resolution, commit history, viewing changes, checking repository state, and git workflows.

skills.name: doc-updater
skills.description: Maintain {{INSTRUCTION_FILE}} (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) and the three generated convention skills (code-conventions, multi-file-workflows, testing-conventions) accuracy after code changes. Surgical updates only; defers descriptive content to /wiki-refresh and graph maintenance to ensure-context.sh.

skills.name: wiki-ingest-external-docs
skills.description: Stage external descriptive docs (PDFs, DOCX, HTML, Notion/Confluence exports, GitHub issues, images) into docs/llm-wiki/raw/external/ following Karpathy's LLM-Wiki pattern. Content-addressed, manifest-indexed, export-first. Opt-in via wiki.cache_external in framework-config.json. wiki-refresh consumes the manifest on the next run.

skills.description: Project-specific coding conventions, gotchas, and WRONG/CORRECT examples (generated during initialization)

skills.name: multi-file-workflows
skills.description: Ordered checklists for cross-cutting changes — add endpoint, add entity, etc. (generated during initialization)

skills.name: testing-conventions
skills.description: Project-specific testing conventions, fixtures, mocking rules, and examples (generated during initialization)

skills.name: mastering-react-native-skill
skills.description: Orientation skill for React Native and Expo projects. Routes work to upstream marketplace plugins (Callstack, Software Mansion) and applies Qubika conventions

skills.name: mastering-expo-skill
skills.description: Orientation skill for Expo projects. Routes work to the upstream expo@expo-plugins marketplace plugin (EAS, Expo Router, expo-module, deployment, upgrade, etc.)

skills.name: qubika-data-contracts
skills.description: Define, enforce, and govern data contracts using Ontos — covering ODCS contract lifecycle, governance DSL automation, and data product ownership

skills.name: qubika-data-quality
skills.description: Add data quality validation to Qubika pipelines using DQX (primary framework), Delta constraints, and DLT expectations

skills.name: qubika-databricks-ai-functions
skills.description: Use Databricks SQL AI functions (`ai_query`, `ai_classify`, `ai_extract`, `ai_summarize`) for Gold-layer LLM enrichment — with Qubika's cost guardrails, model-picking conventions, and watermarked re-run patterns. Read when enriching tabular data with an LLM in SQL rather than Python.

skills.name: qubika-databricks-sdk
skills.description: Use the Databricks Python SDK following Qubika conventions — profile-based auth, named catalogs, async-safe call patterns. Foundation skill for any Python code that talks to Databricks (jobs, UC, SQL, serving, secrets).

skills.name: qubika-databricks-vector-search
skills.description: Build and query Databricks Vector Search indexes for RAG on Qubika data. Covers Delta Sync indexes from Delta tables, SQL `vector_search()` + Python SDK queries, metadata filters for tenancy isolation, hybrid search, and the reindex flow when embedding models change. Read when standing up RAG / similarity search on top of Gold or curated data.

skills.name: qubika-federated-ingestion
skills.description: Ingest data from external databases (SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Redshift, Synapse) into Databricks using Lakefed-Ingest metadata-driven orchestration

skills.name: qubika-jira-workflow
skills.description: Read Jira tickets from the Qubika DE board and translate them into Databricks pipeline tasks, following the standard ticket-to-code workflow

skills.name: qubika-kafka-patterns
skills.description: Kafka-specific patterns for Databricks streaming — Schema Registry + Avro, offset / DLQ / dedup strategies, EventHub Kafka-protocol mode, schema evolution, and the gotchas that bite in production. Read when integrating Kafka beyond a single hello-world topic.

skills.name: qubika-lakebridge
skills.description: Transpile legacy SQL (Snowflake, Oracle, Teradata, T-SQL, DataStage) to Databricks SQL and validate data accuracy after migration using Lakebridge

skills.name: qubika-medallion-architecture
skills.description: Build Bronze/Silver/Gold data lake pipelines following Qubika's medallion architecture conventions on Databricks Delta Lake

skills.name: qubika-monitoring-observability
skills.description: Set up observability for Databricks pipelines — job alerts, data freshness monitoring, SLA tracking, and incident response patterns

skills.name: qubika-saas-api-ingestion
skills.description: Ingest data from SaaS REST APIs (HubSpot, Salesforce, Zendesk, Stripe) into Bronze using secret-scope auth, cursor pagination, incremental watermarks, and retry/backoff

skills.name: qubika-streaming-pipelines
skills.description: Build real-time data pipelines on Databricks using Auto Loader, Spark Structured Streaming, and Kafka integration following Qubika conventions

skills.name: qubika-uc-migration
skills.description: Migrate Hive Metastore tables and workspace assets to Unity Catalog using UCX, and replicate across workspaces with Databricks-Replicator

skills.name: qubika-unity-catalog-governance
skills.description: Create and govern Unity Catalog resources (catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes) following Qubika's naming, tagging, and access control conventions
